rulings.Case Title: DOUGLAS ALAN GREENBERG VS. ASSESSMENT APPEALS BOARD OF THE CITY AND COUNTY ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER to Amended PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE/ PROHIBITION/ CERTIFICATION
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for September 12, 2024 line 1. Defendant's Demurrer to Amended Petition for Writ of Mandate is SUSTAINED with leave to amend. As the Court previously ruled the only procedural mechanism to seek the requested relief by the Petitioner it has to be made in conjunction with a request for refund. Additionally, the Court finds that the timing of the filing of the writ under the circumstances of this case is governed by Cal. Code Regs., tit. 18, 325. Therefore, Plaintiff must in good faith allege: (1) that Plaintiff complied with Cal. Code Regs., tit. 18, 325, subd. (a)(3) ("If so requested by an applicant or an applicant's agent, the determination shall become final upon issuance of the findings of fact Such a request must be made by the applicant or the applicant's agent prior to or at the conclusion of the hearing.") and (2) that Plaintiff paid taxes and complied with administrative procedures regarding the refund for 2021, 2022, 2023, and 2024. If plaintiff takes the position advanced in the opposition that this is not an action for refund and that the issue is not "ripe," the amendment to conform to the procedures under Main & Von Karman Associates v. County of Orange (1994) 23 Cal.App.4th 337, is not made in good faith. If Plaintiff intends to amend in accordance with this ruling, all amendments must be made in good faith. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: NAM SHI LEE VS. EUGENE JUN KWOCK TOM ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE RE PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for September 12, 2024 line 4. OSC RE: Preliminary Injunction is DENIED without prejudice. Based on the operative complaint and evidence submitted in support of the application for an Order to Show Cause, the moving party failed to show any probability of prevailing on the merits, having provided Trustee's Deed upon Sale for 3249 25th Steet. Notice of errata filed 6 days before the hearing, and 4 days after the opposition was filed, is not considered. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). rulings.Case Title: HANCHEN ZHANG VS. EDWIN LAI ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for September 12, 2024 line 7. Defendant's Demurrer to Amended Complaint is continued to September 20, 2024 for Plaintiff to provide a declaration attaching a copy of the First Amended Complaint with the following allegations tabbed and highlighted: (1) the specific subpart of CC 1946.2 supporting why tenancy is exempt from TPA 2019; (2) allegations that SFRO applies to this tenancy; (3) allegations regarding service of Form 1007. The declaration shall be limited to attachment of the true and correct copy of the First Amended Complaint with such First Amended Complaint attached containing 3 highlighted portions and corresponding tabs 1-3. Declaration shall be filed and courtesy copies delivered no later than September 16, 2024 with a cover letter reflecting new hearing date. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849).
